steps.title: Identify the grammatical requirement
steps.content: The blank needs a possessive pronoun that matches the number of its antecedent. The antecedent is “the North Sea,” which is singular.

steps.title: Apply the singular possessive form
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. The antecedent is "the North Sea" — singular and inanimate. A possessive (not a contraction) is needed before the noun "shores." Rule: use the singular possessive "its" for singular inanimate antecedents.
  • Choice A — "it's": Contraction of "it is" — not a possessive. Reads as "the North Sea it is shores," which is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — "its": Singular possessive pronoun. Correctly refers to the singular antecedent "the North Sea." ✓
  • Choice C — "their": Plural possessive — wrong number for a singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — "they're": Contraction of "they are" — not a possessive and plural. Both errors. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
